template:
**DOCUMENT FORMAT — MASSACHUSETTS DEED**:
Instrument of conveyance governed by M.G.L. c. 183 (Recording of Deeds and Other Instruments). Three statutory deed forms are recognized in Massachusetts: (1) the Massachusetts QUITCLAIM DEED (most common — gives covenants AGAINST THE GRANTOR'S OWN ACTS under c. 183, § 11; stronger than a 'quitclaim' elsewhere), (2) the WARRANTY DEED (gives full common-law covenants of seisin, good right to convey, freedom from encumbrances, quiet enjoyment, and warranty under c. 183, § 10), and (3) the RELEASE DEED (no covenants — only releases whatever interest grantor may have). Must meet execution requirements of c. 183, §§ 6–8: in writing, signed, acknowledged before a notary, sealed. Recording in the county Registry of Deeds (recorded land) or Registry District of the Land Court (registered land under c. 185) perfects notice to third parties. Target length: 1–2 pages. Subject to Deed Excise Tax under M.G.L. c. 64D, § 1 at the statutory rate of $4.56 per $1,000 of consideration (rounded up).

**DYNAMIC BEHAVIOR — READ documentOptions**:
- `deedType`: `quitclaim` (default — MA statutory form) | `warranty` (full covenants) | `release` (no covenants — typically intra-family or cure).
- `grantorIsEntity`: boolean — when true, use entity-execution block (LLC Manager, corporate officer with corporate-vote reference).
- `grantorIsTrust`: boolean — when true, include Trustee's Certificate reference under M.G.L. c. 184, § 35.
- `grantorIsMarried`: boolean — when true and property is homestead, address spousal-release/homestead-waiver issue under c. 188.
- `registeredLand`: boolean — when true, use Land Court document formatting and reference Certificate of Title.
- `homesteadDeclaration`: boolean — when true, include reference to any existing c. 188 automatic or declared homestead; if grantor is MA primary residence seller, flag for discharge.
- `consideration`: string — amount of consideration; used to compute deed excise tax.
- `nominalConsideration`: boolean — when true (e.g., gift, intra-family), use 'nominal consideration of $1.00' language and note tax exemption rationale.

**STRUCTURE**:

[Recording margin / top space preserved for Registry of Deeds stamp]

## [DEED TYPE — e.g., MASSACHUSETTS QUITCLAIM DEED]

### GRANTOR
[Grantor full legal name], [of/having an address at] [grantor address], [city/town], [County] County, [State], [marital status if individual — e.g., married/unmarried/single/widow(er)] [if entity: 'a [type of entity] organized under the laws of [jurisdiction], with a principal place of business at [address]'],

**for consideration paid** of [$consideration or 'ONE DOLLAR AND OTHER VALUABLE CONSIDERATION'], the receipt and sufficiency of which is hereby acknowledged,

### GRANTS TO
[Grantee full legal name(s)], [of/having an address at] [grantee address], [city/town], [County] County, [State], [marital status / tenancy: e.g., 'as tenants by the entirety' / 'as joint tenants with rights of survivorship' / 'as tenants in common'],

### WITH [warranty / quitclaim / release] COVENANTS
[Based on `deedType`]:
- **warranty**: 'with WARRANTY COVENANTS' (triggers full statutory covenants under c. 183, § 10).
- **quitclaim**: 'with QUITCLAIM COVENANTS' (triggers covenants against grantor's own acts under c. 183, § 11).
- **release**: (omit 'with [X] covenants' — release deed simply grants and releases without covenants).

the land in [city/town], [County] County, Massachusetts, described as follows:

### LEGAL DESCRIPTION
[Insert exact metes-and-bounds legal description OR reference to recorded plan OR unit/condominium description. For registered land, reference the Certificate of Title.]

Being the same premises conveyed to Grantor by deed dated [prior date] and recorded in the [County] Registry of Deeds in Book [book], Page [page] [or, for registered land: 'being the same premises described in Certificate of Title No. [cert number] filed with the [District] Registry District of the Land Court as Document No. [doc number]'].

[Include any recorded easements, restrictions, and appurtenances to be conveyed; and any 'subject to' clauses for existing encumbrances.]

### [IF homesteadDeclaration OR grantorIsMarried] HOMESTEAD
[If grantor has declared homestead under c. 188:] Grantor acknowledges that the conveyance of the Premises operates as a release of any Declaration of Homestead recorded at [Book/Page], consistent with M.G.L. c. 188, § 10. [If grantor is married, even without declared homestead, the automatic homestead under c. 188, § 4 is terminated as to this Property upon conveyance.] [If grantor is married and property is primary residence but homestead exists in the non-signing spouse's name:] The non-signing spouse [name] joins in this Deed for the purpose of releasing any homestead interest.

### EXECUTION

Executed as a sealed instrument this _____ day of ________________, 20___.

[Based on `grantorIsEntity` / `grantorIsTrust`]:

**Individual Grantor**:
_______________________________
[Grantor full legal name]

[If married with homestead release:]
_______________________________
[Spouse full legal name], spouse of Grantor, joining solely to release homestead rights

**Entity Grantor**:
[Entity name]

By: _______________________________
Name: [Officer name]
Title: [e.g., Manager, President]
Duly authorized, as evidenced by the [resolution / vote / certificate] attached hereto or recorded herewith.

**Trust Grantor**:
[Trust name]

By: _______________________________
[Trustee name], Trustee
[Pursuant to Trustee's Certificate recorded with this Deed under M.G.L. c. 184, § 35.]

### ACKNOWLEDGMENT

COMMONWEALTH OF MASSACHUSETTS
[COUNTY] COUNTY, ss.

On this _____ day of ________________, 20___, before me, the undersigned notary public, personally appeared [Grantor name], proved to me through satisfactory evidence of identification, being [form of ID], to be the person whose name is signed on the preceding or attached document, and acknowledged to me that [he/she/they] signed it voluntarily for its stated purpose [add 'as Trustee' / 'as Manager' / etc., as applicable].

_______________________________
Notary Public
Printed name: ________________
My commission expires: ________________

### [Recording Block — Registry of Deeds use]
AFTER RECORDING, RETURN TO:
[Closing attorney name + firm + address]

PROPERTY ADDRESS: [street, city/town, ZIP]

GRANTOR'S ADDRESS: [address]
GRANTEE'S ADDRESS: [address]

**CONSTRAINTS**:
- Cite M.G.L. exactly: `M.G.L. c. 183` (recording), `c. 183, §§ 6–8` (execution requirements), `c. 183, § 10` (warranty covenants), `c. 183, § 11` (quitclaim covenants — AGAINST GRANTOR'S ACTS), `c. 184, § 35` (trustee's certificate), `c. 185` (registered land), `c. 188` (homestead), `c. 188, § 4` (automatic homestead), `c. 188, § 10` (release of declared homestead), `c. 64D, § 1` (deed excise tax — $4.56 / $1,000).
- MA QUITCLAIM DEED IS NOT the same as a 'quitclaim' in most other states. MA quitclaim gives covenants against grantor's own acts under c. 183, § 11 — do NOT describe it as 'no covenants.'
- Deed MUST be (a) in writing, (b) signed, (c) acknowledged (notarized), and (d) sealed. Absence of any element risks unrecordability. 'As a sealed instrument' is the operative seal language in MA.
- Registered land under M.G.L. c. 185 uses DIFFERENT recording procedures — deeds to registered land must be filed, not recorded, and the Certificate of Title must be amended. Reference the Land Court Registry District, not the Registry of Deeds.
- Deed Excise Tax under c. 64D, § 1 at $4.56 per $1,000 is paid upon recording (some municipalities add a Community Preservation Act surcharge). Check [municipality] for any local surcharge.
- EXEMPTIONS from deed excise tax (c. 64D, §§ 1, 2) include: deeds to/from government, deeds of correction, most gift and intra-family deeds (but SCRUTINY by Registry is common).
- Massachusetts recognizes automatic homestead protection of up to $125,000 under c. 188, § 4 AND declared homestead of up to $500,000 under c. 188, § 3. A married grantor conveying a home must address homestead release (either by express language or by spouse's joinder).
- For Tenancy by the Entirety (married couple): include 'as tenants by the entirety' language; TBE gives full survivorship and creditor protection under c. 209, § 1.
- ALWAYS include 'Being the same premises' clause with the prior-deed book/page reference — REBA Title Standards require it for record chain.
- Use precise legal description; if unsure, reference the recorded plan or Certificate of Title — do NOT draft metes and bounds absent a survey.
- Target length: 1–2 pages.
hints: Massachusetts Deed. Three statutory forms: Quitclaim (c. 183 § 11 — covenants against grantor's own acts — STRONGER than CA/TX quitclaim, most common MA form), Warranty (c. 183 § 10 — full covenants), Release (no covenants). Execution: written + signed + acknowledged + sealed ('as a sealed instrument') per c. 183 §§ 6–8. Recorded land vs. Registered land (c. 185 — Land Court Certificate of Title). Deed excise tax $4.56/$1,000 per c. 64D § 1. Homestead: automatic c. 188 § 4 ($125k), declared c. 188 § 3 ($500k); release per c. 188 § 10. Tenancy by entirety for married couples (c. 209 § 1 — creditor protection). Trust grantor: Trustee's Certificate under c. 184 § 35. REBA Title Standards require 'being the same premises' + prior book/page. Intra-family deeds exempt from excise per c. 64D § 1.
